text: Welcome to This is My Architecture. My name is Richard and today I have Pablo
from Love Bonito with us. So Pablo could you tell us what Love Bonito does?
So Love Bonito is the largest omnichannel brand in the region for women in fashion.
But we also have presence in Australia and US. Okay so on the board itself we
have different components and services that are being presented here. So could
you tell us your use case and also walk us through the architecture diagram for us
please. So our web application, our e-commerce is actually powered by Magento
itself. Magento is an e-commerce platform. This architecture is adapt to make
Magento on the best way possible. So in our case all the users are going straight
to CloudFront. And then we are going to use Varnish in EC2 in order to cache all the
content or as much as possible from Magento itself. Then the traffic is going to be
forward to EKS, Elastic Kubernetes Service. From Elastic Kubernetes Service we
are going to host all our Magento applications but other services as well.
Those images, those docker images are going to be pulled from ECR, Elastic Container Registry.
Okay so how about the Aurora and Elastic cache? What roles do they play in this
architecture with Magento? Right so Aurora MySQL has actually all our business data.
We have a real replica there with a multi-AC that allow us to contain all the
information that the business requires. Elastic cache for the other side we are
using Redis and we are caching as much as possible the content from MySQL in
Elastic cache using Redis. Okay so with that being said I see S3 on the left corner of the
diagram left out. So what role does S3 play in delivering this application?
Right when we start migrating this application the current version at the
moment for Magento wasn't supporting natively S3 as a service. So we are
actually using a service called S3Fuse to mount inside of our instances S3
bucket. So on this case we are going to have as a part of the file system our S3
bucket. Okay this sounds really innovative. So with this S3 being mounted as your
file system what values does it bring for you in delivering contents to end
customers? So all the content that is going to S3 is going to be actually
forwarded or provided by CloudFront itself. This is going to make it faster
for the user all around the world on the different areas that we are providing
service getting the content as much as fast as possible. Okay that sounds really
interesting. So right now I think Elastic Couranty Service is something that it
stands out the most. So what were your design choices that you made in moving
your Magento application to Elastic Couranty Service and what were the benefits from that?
Right. On the previous platform we couldn't actually deploy as often as we
want and we couldn't deploy without having downtime on our website. When we
started exploring different ways to set up the new architecture we realized that
with our small team the best way to do it is using Kubernetes but also to do all
the rest of the configurations regarding services setting up the rules for
auto scaling it was a lot better using Kubernetes. That's why we decide to use
Kubernetes. For the older side we also we are using Magento but Magento can
actually be a split on three different parts. Magento itself is a monolithic
application but at the end of the day you can split between Chrome jobs, the admin
panel and the web application itself. In our case we can put a specific rules for the
web applications for auto scaling, we can put the specific hardware for the admin
panel that is not the same for the web application.
Okay so as part of the benefit from what you have shared with us previously you had
a successful sales that were ran off from this architecture. So could you tell us a
bit more about that? Right so after we did the migration the business was to start
realizing that whenever we have a sale doesn't mean that we are going to have a
downtime because now we can adapt the traffic for the different sales and for
the different amount of operations that we are going to have on the business.
All right so we all with the successful migrations and lessons learned from
migrating your workload what would be your next steps for this architecture and the
journey with us? Right so the architecture is going to shape a bit on the future
because we are going to roll out a headless application removing the Magento web
application part but also creating a set of different Node.js API's to support the
headless part as well. All right that's really interesting thank you for your time
Pablo it's really appreciated and thank you everyone for joining us today this is
my architecture hope to see you again soon
